dedupe_key.symptom: cross-tenant read of site config and stored credential metadata
dedupe_key.root_cause: SiteController.show loads Site by id without scoping to the authenticated tenant

why_abusable_under_preconditions: show() resolves the Site via Site::findOrFail($id) and never authorizes it against the caller's tenant (no SitePolicy::view gate, no whereBelongsTo(tenant) scope). Under the preconditions below, any authenticated tenant can read another tenant's site configuration and credential metadata by enumerating sequential site ids. Framed as a failing invariant: 'a site is only readable by its owning tenant' does not hold here. No exploit payload — the defect is the absent scope check.

preconditions.required_role/session: any authenticated BearHost user with a valid session
preconditions.tenant/account setup: two tenants exist; the victim tenant owns a target site id distinct from the attacker's
preconditions.impact: read of another tenant's site configuration and stored-credential metadata (cross-tenant isolation break)

dedupe_key.symptom: a captured control-plane command can be replayed against a connected WP install
dedupe_key.root_cause: DispatchSiteCommand signs the payload but does not include or verify a per-message nonce/timestamp, so signatures are valid indefinitely

why_abusable_under_preconditions: Commands are HMAC-signed over {site_id, action, args} but omit a nonce and timestamp, and descry's verifier checks only the signature. The invariant 'each control-plane command executes at most once on the target install' is not enforced. Given the preconditions, an observed signed command (e.g. a benign re-sync) can be re-sent and will re-execute. This is replay exposure, not an injection — no offensive tooling implied; remediation is binding a nonce+expiry into the signed payload and rejecting reuse on the descry side.

preconditions.required_role/session: network position able to observe or capture a previously issued signed command (e.g. a compromised egress proxy or a logged transcript)
preconditions.tenant/account setup: a connected site with descry agent installed and command channel enabled
preconditions.affected path: control-plane -> WP command channel (DispatchSiteCommand -> descry agent)
preconditions.impact: re-execution of a prior remote action on a customer install

dedupe_key.symptom: operators backtrack to find the reject-reason field, adding steps to every triage
dedupe_key.root_cause: reject action opens the reason input below the fold with no scroll/focus, so the primary action appears disabled

evidence: screenshot: staging /content-gate item triage — reject button enabled but reason field off-screen
measured: agency-admin persona completed reject in 9 steps vs the 5-step success_criteria; 2 backtracks and one mis-click on the disabled-looking submit across 3 runs
